roles.name: Alex Chen
roles.title: Senior Product Manager
roles.description: Confident, credited, visible. His ideas are heard, his authority unquestioned.

roles.name: Sarah Chen
roles.title: Senior Product Manager
roles.description: Same role. Same skills. Interrupted, overlooked, and doubted every step of the way.

roles.name: Jamie
roles.title: Product Intern
roles.description: Invisible, underestimated, and routinely ignored. The ideas still come. The credit doesn't.

scenarios.title: The Strategy Meeting
scenarios.description: The quarterly product strategy meeting. Everyone is presenting their roadmap ideas.

male-manager.content: You present the Q3 roadmap. The room is attentive. When you finish, Mark says, 'Solid thinking, Alex. Let's move forward with your vision.' Three heads nod in agreement.
male-manager.quote: Solid thinking. Let's move forward with your vision.

female-employee.content: You begin presenting the Q3 roadmap. Halfway through your second slide, David cuts in — 'Actually, what Sarah is trying to say is...' and rephrases your exact idea. The room listens to him instead.
female-employee.quote: Actually, what Sarah is trying to say is...

intern.content: You raise your hand to contribute a key insight. Mark glances at you briefly, then looks back at his laptop. Your point goes unacknowledged. Ten minutes later, David says the same thing — to applause.

scenarios.title: Performance Feedback Email
scenarios.description: Your manager sends you your quarterly performance review via email.

male-manager.subject: Q3 Review — Exceptional Performance
male-manager.body:
Alex,

Your leadership during the Q3 launch was remarkable. Your decisive thinking and ability to align the team made all the difference. I'm recommending you for the Senior Director track.

Let's grab coffee to discuss your growth path.

Best,
Mark

female-employee.subject: Q3 Review — Performance Notes
female-employee.body:
Sarah,

You did well this quarter. A few things to keep in mind — the team responds better when you're less assertive in meetings. Perhaps soften your communication style. Your emotional responses during the retrospective were noted.

Happy to chat if you have questions.

Mark

intern.subject: Internship Check-In
intern.body:
Hi Jamie,

Just a routine check-in. How are you settling in? Let us know if you need anything.

Hope you're enjoying the experience!

HR

scenarios.title: Team Slack Thread
scenarios.description: A team Slack discussion breaks out about a critical product decision.

messages.text: I think we should delay the launch by two weeks for QA.
That makes total sense. Better to ship right than ship fast.
Agreed. Alex always thinks ahead. +1 on the delay.
Locking it in. Two week delay. Thanks for catching this, Alex.
I think we should delay the launch by two weeks for QA.
Hmm, not sure. We have stakeholder pressure.
Actually, I've been thinking — what if we delay launch by two weeks for QA? Just to make sure we ship quality.
That's smart David. Let's do it. Great call.

female-employee.outcome: Idea stolen, credit given to male colleague

messages.text: Should we consider a two-week QA delay?
Good discussion everyone. Let's table this for now.

scenarios.title: Promotion Conversation
scenarios.description: A one-on-one with your manager about career advancement.

male-manager.content: Mark leans forward. 'Alex, we've been watching your trajectory. The VP slot is opening up. I want to nominate you — you have the presence and potential.' He hands you a Leadership Development roadmap.
male-manager.quote: You have the presence and the potential.

female-employee.content: Mark smiles warmly. 'Sarah, you've been doing great. Maybe in a few years when you're ready...' You ask about the VP opening. 'We need someone who can really command the room, you know? But keep growing!' The conversation ends before you can respond.
female-employee.quote: Maybe in a few years when you're ready...

intern.content: You requested a 1:1 with Mark two weeks ago. His assistant finally responds: 'Mark's calendar is fully booked. Could you speak to your supervisor instead?' Your direct message to Mark goes unread for 6 days.

interventions.title: Structured Meeting Agendas
interventions.description: All participants are given equal time slots on the agenda. Interruptions are tracked.

interventions.title: Blind Credit Attribution
interventions.description: Ideas are recorded with timestamps and author tags before meetings to ensure proper credit.

interventions.title: Inclusive Language Policy
interventions.description: Feedback templates replace gendered language. Words like 'assertive' and 'emotional' are flagged.
interventions.impact: Reduces gendered language in reviews by 82%

interventions.title: Sponsorship Program
interventions.description: Representatives from underrepresented groups are actively sponsored for advancement opportunities.
